title: Web-uplift audit report
description: A modern-UX audit report for a single site: the explored paths, the findings (each tied to a violated principle and/or a Modern Web Guidance use case), and a prioritised task list.

mode.description: report = critique only; fix = source fixes were applied and the audit re-run

status.description: blocked = bot wall / login required; partial = some paths failed

evidenceUsed.description: The evidence modalities and tools the model actually ran for this audit (e.g. 'screenshot', 'layout-metrics', 'transition-video', 'heap-summary', 'dom+source', 'evaluate-probe', 'lighthouse', 'axe-core'). Makes the audit honest about its method, since method selection is the model's at inspection time, not pre-baked.

artifacts.description: The concrete evidence files the audit produced (the artifacts manifest). Each entry ties an artifact type to its on-disk path (relative to the report directory) and a caption. report.md embeds screenshots inline and links the rest (video/trace/har/heap/lighthouse). Findings reference these paths via finding.artifacts so each 'fix this' task is backed by concrete before-evidence.

type.description: What kind of artifact this is, matching the evidence primitive that produced it.

path.description: Path to the artifact, relative to the report directory (e.g. 'evidence/no-dark-mode-dark.png', 'evidence/trace-summary.json').

caption.description: Short human-readable description of what the artifact shows and under which condition.

condition.description: Emulated condition the artifact was captured under (e.g. 'prefers-color-scheme: dark', 'viewport: 360x800'), if any.

findingIds.description: Findings this artifact evidences (the inverse of finding.artifacts; either or both may be populated).

config.description: The web-uplift.json project config the audit honoured, if present (see schema/config.schema.json). Records declared scope/opt-outs/intent so the report is honest about what the developer asked for.

loaded.description: Whether a web-uplift.json was found and applied.

principleOutcomes.description: Per-principle applicability + verdict, so the audit reports 'opted-out' and 'n/a (judged)' DISTINCTLY from pass/issue. This is the 'quality without shaming' record: a contextual principle that legitimately does not apply is not penalised, but the rationale is captured.

status.description: pass = met; issues = one or more findings; not-applicable = the model judged the principle does not apply (contextual, no declared intent); opted-out = the developer declared an opt-out in web-uplift.json.

reason.description: REQUIRED when status is not-applicable (the model's judgement-call rationale) or opted-out (the developer's declared reason, echoed from web-uplift.json).

paths.description: The user paths / test cases exercised (mirrors the test plan).

conditions.description: Emulated conditions evaluated for this path (e.g. 'prefers-color-scheme: dark', 'viewport: 360x800', 'keyboard-only').

pathId.description: Which exercised path this finding came from.

url.description: Specific URL the finding applies to.

principleId.description: Principle violated, from knowledge/principles.json (e.g. adapt-to-the-user). Optional: a finding may be guidance-only.

principleCheckId.description: The specific check within the principle (e.g. respects-color-scheme).

guidanceId.description: Modern Web Guidance use-case id that addresses this (from `modern-web-guidance search`/`list`), e.g. dark-mode.

guidanceCategory.description: Guidance category (e.g. user-experience, performance, accessibility, css, forms).

summary.description: What diverges from the modern approach, in one or two sentences.

evidence.description: Concrete observation backing the finding (e.g. 'No color-scheme declared; background stays #fff under prefers-color-scheme: dark', or 'horizontal scrollbar at 360px viewport; .hero has width: 1200px').

artifacts.description: Artifact paths (matching entries in the report-level `artifacts` manifest) that concretely evidence this finding, so the report ties each 'action to fix' to its before-evidence (e.g. the dark-mode screenshot, the reduced-motion video, the trace summary). Paths are relative to the report directory.

suggestedFix.description: Concrete remediation following Modern Web Guidance, not a restatement of the problem (e.g. 'Add color-scheme: light dark and use light-dark() for surface colors; see guidance id dark-mode').

effort.description: Rough implementation effort, used for prioritisation.

taskList.description: Prioritised, deduplicated list of fixes derived from the findings (highest leverage first). The unit of work handed to fix mode.

status.description: Updated by fix mode as the hill-climb progresses.

auditPasses.description: How many audit passes ran (fix mode re-audits, so >1).
